steps.text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level

causal_links.mechanism: Boiling occurs when the vapor pressure of the water equals the surrounding atmospheric pressure.

causal_links.to: Temperature of water remains at 100 degrees Celsius (at sea level)
causal_links.mechanism: During boiling, the energy input is used to change the state of water from liquid to gas, rather than increasing the temperature. This maintains the temperature at the boiling point until all the water has evaporated.
